title: linum brevifolium f. squamifolium
intro: Linum brevifolium f. squamifolium, also known as the prickly flax or short-leaved flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant with short leaves and a prickly texture. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum brevifolium f. squamifolium include relieving pain and inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
